_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 4-8 Safe Transportation of Children in Ambulances (Revised 01/27/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — no MCA drug selections required.
condition: Safe Transportation of Children in Ambulances
protocol_reference: Michigan Obstetrics and Pediatrics Protocol Section 4-8: Safe Transportation of Children in Ambulances — MDHHS Approved 1/27/23, Reviewed 2023
purpose: Guidelines for safe transportation of children in ambulances. This protocol applies to pediatric patients of a height or weight requiring a child restraint system and identifies ambulance child restraints as the expected transport method unless unavailable, unachievable, or detrimental.

sections.title: Definitions
points: Child Restraint System (CRS): a device designed for child safety in transportation, including vehicle car seats, forward/rear-facing seats, and booster seats
Ambulance Child Restraint (ACR): a subset of CRS designed for use in ambulances and on ambulance stretchers; ACR is not a brand name
An ACR does not include car seats designed for personal vehicles
Devices meeting the ACR definition and approved by the MCA may be utilized

sections.title: Criteria and Standard Procedure
points: Any pediatric patient requiring a CRS and transported in an ambulance must be in an ACR
When not transported in an ACR, this must be documented and reported to the MCA
Transport the patient on the ambulance stretcher secured with an ACR
Consider the child's height and weight when determining the appropriate ACR and follow manufacturer recommendations
When ACR use is unavailable, unachievable, or detrimental, use situational guideline alternatives in order and document/report to the MCA

sections.title: Situation 1 — Uninjured/Not Ill Child Accompanying a Patient
points: Arrange transport in a vehicle other than an emergency ground ambulance in a size-appropriate, properly installed, undamaged CRS
If needed, request an ACR-equipped transporting vehicle
If necessary, transport in the ambulance front passenger seat in a size-appropriate, properly installed, undamaged CRS with airbags off and seat moved fully back
Other alternatives include a forward-facing EMS provider seat/captain's chair or rear-facing EMS provider seat with size-appropriate, properly installed, undamaged CRS

sections.title: Situation 2 and 3 — Ill/Injured Child
points: If the child does not require continuous intensive monitoring or interventions, request an ACR-equipped vehicle if condition allows, or use a size-appropriate undamaged CRS secured appropriately on the ambulance stretcher
If needed, alternatives include forward-facing or rear-facing EMS provider seat with size-appropriate, properly installed, undamaged CRS

If CRS/ACR alternatives are unavailable, secure the child to the ambulance stretcher using three horizontal restraints across chest, pelvis, and lower extremities and one vertical restraint across each shoulder

For a child requiring continuous intensive monitoring or intervention, request an ACR-equipped vehicle if condition allows, otherwise secure to the stretcher with the same restraint method while allowing appropriate medical care

sections.title: Situation 4 and 5 — Spinal Motion Restriction and Multiple Patients
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 4-8: SMR and Multiple Patient Transport
points: For patients requiring spinal motion restriction or lying flat, request an ACR-equipped transporting vehicle and follow Spinal Precautions-Procedure Protocol
If already secured to a spine board and removal would be detrimental, add padding as needed and secure the board to the stretcher
For multiple patient transport, transport each child as a single patient using situations 1 through 4 and use additional units as needed
For mother and newborn, both are patients; prevent newborn hypothermia immediately and continuously
If mother has no complications, transport newborn in an ACR on the ambulance stretcher and mother in the rear-facing EMS provider seat

If mother has delivery complications and needs the stretcher, transport newborn in an approved size-appropriate car seat in the rear-facing EMS provider seat with a belt path that prevents lateral and forward movement under continuous monitoring

deterioration_flags: ACR unavailable, unachievable, or detrimental — use the ordered alternatives, document the reason, and report to the MCA
Child requires continuous intensive monitoring/intervention — secure to stretcher with horizontal and shoulder restraints while maintaining access for care
Mother-newborn transport — treat both as patients and prevent newborn hypothermia continuously
scope_notes: ACR/CRS selection and documentation: all levels
Newborn monitoring and hypothermia prevention during mother-baby transport: all levels
Spinal motion restriction with pediatric device: all levels — per Spinal Motion Restriction-Procedure Protocol
No medications involved in this protocol
Documentation of restraint method used required in patient care report
